 {"id":"gt-fqcz","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: 'Gate timed out'
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Moved from beads
Originally bd-ykqu. Gate notifications are Deacon's job in gastown.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:18.268978-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:18.268978-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-fqcz","depends_on_id":"gt-dh65","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:32.873641-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"gt-frs","title":"Polecat name pooling: Bounded reusable names","description":"Polecats reuse names from a bounded pool (50) with overflow to sequence numbers.

## Naming Scheme
- Pool: polecat-01 through polecat-50 (prefer low numbers)
- Overflow: <rigname>-<sequenceNumber> (e.g., beads-51, gastown-52)

## Design
- Witness tracks which pool names are in use
- On spawn: pick first available from pool
- If pool exhausted: use rigname-N format
- On completion: pool name returns, sequence numbers don't

## Why?
- User experience: tmux sessions survive polecat restarts
- Users stay attached, see new polecat start (like mayor respawn loop)
- Bounded resource usage for common case
- Scales beyond 50 when needed

## Implementation
- Witness maintains name allocation in beads or local state
- Tmux session runs respawn loop (like mayor)
- Name released on graceful exit or when witness detects dead session","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.43866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.551244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:29:30.648439-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-g2d","title":"Mayor session cycling prompting","description":"Add session cycling section to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## When to Cycle

Cycle proactively when:
- Running for several hours
- Context feels crowded (losing track of earlier state)
- Major phase completed
- About to start complex new work

## Composing Handoff Notes

1. Gather information:
   town status    # Overall health
   town rigs      # Each rig state
   town inbox     # Pending messages
   bd ready       # Work items

2. Compose note with this structure:

[HANDOFF_TYPE]: mayor_cycle
[TIMESTAMP]: <current time>
[SESSION_DURATION]: <how long running>

## Active Swarms
<per-rig swarm status>

## Rig Status
<table of rig health>

## Pending Escalations
<issues needing your decision>

## In-Flight Decisions
<decisions being made>

## Recent Actions
<last 5-10 things you did>

## Delegated Work
<work sent to refineries>

## User Requests
<pending user asks>

## Next Steps
<what next session should do>

## Warnings/Notes
<critical info for next session>

3. Send handoff:
   town mail send mayor/ -s "Session Handoff" -m "<note>"

4. End session - next instance picks up from handoff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:26.188561-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:07.063607-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g2d","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.361163-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-q6lg","title":"mol-crew-session: Startup/shutdown protocols for crew workers","description":"Crew workers (like joe, max) don't have patrol molecules keeping them fresh. When gt gets rebuilt, they have stale binaries that cause hangs and bugs.

## Problem

- Crew binaries get stale when gt is rebuilt elsewhere
- No automatic pull/rebase/rebuild on session start
- No standardized shutdown protocol (sync, push, handoff)

## Solution: mol-crew-session

A molecule template for crew sessions:

### Startup Phase
1. `git pull --rebase` - get latest code
2. `bd sync` - sync beads
3. `go build -o gt ./cmd/gt` - rebuild gt (if in gastown)
4. `gt prime` - load context

### Work Phase  
- Open-ended human interaction
- No molecule steps - just work until done

### Shutdown Phase
1. `git status` - check for uncommitted changes
2. `bd sync` - sync beads
3. `git push` - push code
4. Handoff if incomplete work

## Implementation

1. Define mol-crew-session in builtin_molecules.go
2. Update crew CLAUDE.md to reference the protocol
3. Optionally: gt prime auto-runs startup steps

## Dependencies

- Should implement after deacon/witness/polecat patrols are stable
- Consider: gt-3x0z.10 (Witness patrol molecules)

## Related

- gt-3x0z.9: Deacon wisp patrol (done)
- fix-gt script: Current workaround for binary freshness","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:48:25.658692-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T02:48:25.658692-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-q8du","title":"Configuration in Beads: use pinned beads for rig/agent config","description":"## Summary

Move configuration from JSON files into Beads data plane using pinned beads.

## Motivation

- Config becomes versionable and syncable like issues
- Agents can read config via `bd show`
- Unified data model for everything
- Supports the 'Beads as Universal Data Plane' vision (gt-aqm)

## Config Types to Migrate

### Rig-level (pinned per rig)
- Theme/colors for tmux status line
- Default branch naming conventions
- Merge queue settings
- Witness thresholds

### Agent-level (pinned per role)
- Handoffs (already planned - gt-cu7r)
- Agent preferences
- Current focus/context

### Town-level (pinned at town root)
- Cross-rig settings
- Federation config
- Global themes

## Implementation

1. Implement StatusPinned (beads-6v2) ✓ in progress
2. Create config pinned beads on rig init
3. Add `bd config get/set` commands that read/write pinned beads
4. Migrate existing config.json fields

## Naming Convention

```
<prefix>-cfg-<scope>
gt-cfg-theme      # gastown theme
bd-cfg-merge      # beads merge settings
gm-cfg-town       # town-level config
```

## Dependencies

- beads-6v2: Add StatusPinned to beads schema
- gm-w13: Pinned Beads epic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:59.898116-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:58:59.898116-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qao","title":"CLI: mayor commands (start, attach, stop, status)","description":"Mayor management CLI commands.

## Commands

### gt mayor start
```
gt mayor start [--continue] [--agent AGENT]
```
- --continue: Resume from previous session (check for handoff mail)
- --agent: claude (default) or other agent type

### gt mayor attach
```
gt mayor attach
```
Attach to running Mayor session.

### gt mayor stop
```
gt mayor stop [--grace-period N]
```
Stop Mayor session with optional grace period.

### gt mayor status
```
gt mayor status [--json]
```
Show Mayor running status.

## Session Management
Session name: `gt-mayor`
Working directory: `<town>/mayor/rig/` or `<town>/mayor/`

## Implementation
Similar to session commands but for special Mayor context.

```go
func getMayorPath(townRoot string) string {
    return filepath.Join(townRoot, "mayor")
}

func getMayorSessionName() string {
    return "gt-mayor"
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/mayor.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/mayor_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt mayor start launches Mayor session
- [ ] gt mayor attach works
- [ ] gt mayor stop with grace period
- [ ] gt mayor status shows running state","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:54.721035-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:45.226324-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-vjw","title":"Swarm learning: Session cleanup missing from swarm workflow","description":"## Problem

After Enders Game swarm completed (18 issues merged), 16 polecat sessions were left running but idle. No automated cleanup occurred.

## What Should Happen

1. Witness detects polecat completed work (idle at prompt)
2. Witness verifies git state is clean
3. Witness shuts down session
4. Witness reports completion to Mayor

## GGT Components

- gt-cxx: Witness context cycling (covers self-cycling)
- gt-u1j.9: Witness daemon heartbeat loop
- gt-kmn.6: Witness swarm landing protocol

## Recommendation

Add to Witness responsibilities:
- Monitor for 'work complete' signals (DONE keyword, idle detection)
- Automated session shutdown after verification
- Swarm completion reporting to Mayor

See also: architecture.md 'Worker Cleanup (Witness-Owned)' section which describes this but it wasn't implemented in PGT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:27:52.796587-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:37.409965-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:37.409965-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-vmk7","title":"Guardrail: Verify commits exist before closing polecat issues","description":"When closing an issue that was assigned to a polecat, verify that at least one commit references the issue ID.

## Problem
A polecat can be cleaned up before completing work. If someone then manually closes the issue (or automation does), we lose track that the work was never done.

## Proposed Solution
Add a pre-close check:
```bash
git log --oneline --all --grep='<issue-id>' | head -1
```

If no commits found, warn or block the close.

## Where to Implement
Options:
1. **bd close** - Add --verify flag or make it default for polecat-assigned issues
2. **Witness cleanup** - Verify before clearing assignee
3. **gt polecat done** - Check before marking done

## Related
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol (checks git state, not commits)
- gt polecat git-state: Checks uncommitted work, but 'clean' doesn't mean 'productive'","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:35:24.695717-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:35:24.695717-08:00"}

Check for in_progress issues with no active agent.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:33.988644-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:33.988644-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-vmpo","depends_on_id":"gt-2x79","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:33.989241-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}],"wisp":true}
 {"id":"gt-vnp9","title":"tmux notifications: display-message too subtle, use send-keys instead","description":"## Problem

`tmux display-message` notifications are not visible enough - they appear briefly in the status bar and are easy to miss.

## Current Behavior

router.go uses:
```go
r.tmux.DisplayMessageDefault(sessionID, notification)
```

## What Works

Sending echo commands directly to the terminal:
```bash
tmux send-keys -t <session> "echo '📬 NEW MAIL from mayor'" Enter
```

## Proposed Fix

Change notification method to send visible output to the terminal, perhaps with a box/banner:
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📬 NEW MAIL from mayor
Subject: <subject>
Run: bd mail inbox
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

## Considerations

- This interrupts the terminal output (acceptable for important mail)
- Could check if Claude is mid-response and queue notification
- Or use tmux popup if available","notes":"Additional issues:
1. Enter key not sent properly when chained with send-keys
2. Need to debounce and send Enter separately
3. Correct pattern: send text, sleep briefly, then send Enter","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:35:28.542985-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:29.342381-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:29.342381-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-w3bu","title":"gt spawn: Enter key needs debounce delay after paste","description":"## Problem

When spawning polecats via `gt spawn`, instructions are pasted into tmux but workers often sit idle at prompt because the Enter key arrives before the paste is fully processed.

## Observed Behavior

- Instructions pasted via tmux send-keys
- Enter key sent immediately after
- Worker session shows prompt with no input (paste not submitted)
- Requires manual intervention to nudge workers

## Expected Behavior

- Paste completes fully
- Enter key submits the pasted instructions
- Worker begins executing immediately

## Root Cause

Likely a race condition between paste buffer processing and keypress handling. Need either:
1. Debounce delay before sending Enter
2. Longer delay (Tmax) to ensure paste completes
3. Alternative submission mechanism

## Impact

- Swarm of 10 workers had multiple stalls at startup
- Required manual tmux send-keys to unstick them
- Defeats purpose of automated spawning

## References

- Observed during Mad Max swarm (Dec 18, 2025)
- Affects gt spawn command in internal/cmd/spawn.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:06:50.734123-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00"}
